steps.title: Find the allowed $y$-range at each $x$
steps.content:
A point is a solution only if BOTH $y \lt -5x - 19$ and $y \gt -3x - 12$ hold.
At $x = -5$: need $-3(-5)-12 = 3 \lt y \lt -5(-5)-19 = 6$, so $3 \lt y \lt 6$.
At $x = -6$: need $-3(-6)-12 = 6 \lt y \lt -5(-6)-19 = 11$, so $6 \lt y \lt 11$.
At $x = -8$: need $-3(-8)-12 = 12 \lt y \lt -5(-8)-19 = 21$, so $12 \lt y \lt 21$.

steps.title: Check each table against the ranges
steps.content:
• A) $(-5,6)$: need $3 \lt 6 \lt 6$, but $6 \lt 6$ fails. Eliminate.
  • B) $(-5,4)$: $3 \lt 4 \lt 6$; $(-6,10)$: $6 \lt 10 \lt 11$; $(-8,19)$: $12 \lt 19 \lt 21$. All match.
  • C) $(-5,2)$: need $3 \lt 2$, fails. Eliminate.
  • D) $(-5,1)$: need $3 \lt 1$, fails. Eliminate.
A quick Desmos check is to enter choice B's $x$-values and $y$-values as lists, then test both inequalities at once. That confirms B is correct.
